74895.subject: GLM-5.2: The ChatGPT Moment for Local AI 🤖
74895.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/glm-52-local-ai-guide

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 395k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
Something just shifted in AI, and most people haven’t caught up yet.
For the first time, an open-weight model you can download for free, run on your own hardware, and use under a permissive MIT license is trading head-to-head with the most expensive closed models on the planet.
That model is GLM-5.2 from Z.ai (Zhipu AI; later - just ZAI for simplicity's sake), and the builders who tried it are not being subtle. For example, Matt Velloso, former VP of Meta, DeepMind, & Microsoft, called it “the first open model that passes the bar as a daily driver”. 
Others framed it as the “ChatGPT moment for local AI”, while Jeremy Howard, co-founder of Fast AI, said it is “at least as good as Opus 4.8 and GPT 5.5”.
GLM 5.2 (left) vs. Opus 4.8 (right) building a landing page. Credit: nutlope via X
The numbers back those claims.
GLM-5.2 ships with a 1-million-token context window that can hold an entire codebase at once, tops independent open-weight leaderboards, and ranks as the #1 model in the world for frontend coding on Arena AI once the unavailable Fable model [ https://substack.com/redirect/857ddc27-6736-4547-8798-521b802111f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] is excluded.
And thanks to new dynamic quantization from Unsloth, you can now run it locally on a single 256 GB Mac instead of a datacenter. That’s pretty huge!
The timing here matters a lot. Days before GLM-5.2’s open-weight release, the U.S. Commerce Department ordered Anthropic to suspend global access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 [ https://substack.com/redirect/31d7124d-0d9f-47eb-9d83-0c513b4f8c7b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], leaving non-U.S. developers without their most capable coding models. 
GLM-5.2 therefore stepped into that gap. But we must note ZAI is itself a Beijing-based company on the U.S. Entity List since January 2025, subject to China’s National Intelligence Law. The model’s open weights mitigate some of that risk; the hosted API does not.
This deep dive is the complete playbook on GLM-5.2: how it stacks up against Claude, GPT, and Gemini on real benchmarks, what it costs, how to set it up in Claude Code and Cline, how (and when) to run it locally, the five copy-paste prompts that unlock its full power, and the risks every investor and founder must underwrite before betting on it.
If you build, fund, or compete in AI, understanding the most powerful open model available today is no longer optional.
What Is GLM-5.2?...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_dG9rZW49ZXlKMWMyVnlYMmxrSWpveE1Ea3dOVFV6TURnc0l

>>>>>>>>>> stret

74515.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — June 14
74515.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-14-oleh-bezuhlyi-akqxf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmbZ4FC4IQ0mg0XDN2T%2BrWg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3vrdyj7E8K3ck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqe0o02q~q3-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqe0o02q-q3
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: A July 1 MiCA cutoff looms for millions of EU crypto users while a DPRK-linked $36M hack and a UK naval seizure exposing USDT sanctions evasion show that crypto's geopolitical risk is no longer theoretical. 🚨 Millions of EU Crypto Users Face Exchange Cutoff as MiCA Deadline Hits in Days On July 1, 2026, the temporary operating permission that allowed crypto companies to serve EU customers while awaiting a full MiCA license expires — and exchanges, brokers, and wallet services that have not secured authorization must immediately cut off access to European users with no further grace period. CryptoSlate reports that the deadline creates a live operational crisis for ordinary investors who may lose access to their funds, positions, or platforms overnight, with little notice from providers scrambling to reach compliance. Read full story 💀 Humanity Protocol's $36M Hack Linked to Suspected North Korean Hackers Cybersecurity firm Quantstamp has attributed the $36 million breach of Humanity Protocol to suspected North Korean threat actors, identifying a fraudulent phishing email spoofing crypto exchange Bithumb as the attack's primary entry vector. The incident adds another eight-figure sum to the growing ledger of assets stolen by DPRK-affiliated hacking groups, which have systematically targeted blockchain projects to fund state programs operating under international sanctions. More details ⚖️ CFTC No-Action Letter Opens First Legal Path for Digital Commodity Perpetuals in the US The CFTC issued a staff no-action letter that clears a legal runway for perpetual futures contracts on digital commodities to operate within the United States — a product category that has until now existed almost exclusively at offshore venues to sidestep regulatory uncertainty. The guidance provides immediate operational clarity for US-domiciled exchanges and their institutional clients, who have long been unable to offer the perpetual swap structures that dominate global crypto derivatives volumes. Read more 📊 SEC Proposes Reg NMS Rule Changes That Could Reshape Tokenized Stock Trading The SEC has proposed rescinding Rules 611 and 610e of Regulation NMS — the order-protection and access rules that have governed US equity market structure for two decades — in a move with substantial downstream implications for how tokenized st
